headline: Conflicting signals: Tech optimism clashes with macro inflation concerns
summary: Five AI models delivered sharply divergent predictions on 2026-03-13, with notable consensus splits on major indices and clear tech sector enthusiasm. Claude positioned bullishly on NVDA and QQQ ahead of the GTC conference, while Perplexity warned of pre-market weakness across equities. SPY predictions were particularly fractured, with Claude and Perplexity bearish versus GPT-4o and Grok bullish, reflecting genuine market uncertainty around PCE inflation data.

consensus_picks.note: Complete disagreement: Claude and Perplexity bearish (0.72, 0.7 confidence) vs. GPT-4o and Grok bullish (0.65 each), reflecting macro uncertainty around PCE inflation timing.
Both models bullish on NVDA ahead of GTC 2026 conference, though Grok's $950 target is dramatically more aggressive than Claude's $190.
Both models bearish on Tesla citing supply chain and production issues, though targets differ wildly ($700 vs $220).
Claude bullish on tech rebound (0.68) while Perplexity cautious on NASDAQ weakness (0.65 confidence down).

best_call.summary: Claude's highest-confidence NVDA bull call (0.78) with logical catalyst reasoning around GTC 2026 conference positioning, supported by secondary agreement from Grok.

worst_call.summary: Grok's NVDA target of $950 appears implausibly aggressive relative to current market fundamentals and lacks detailed supporting reasoning compared to Claude's more measured $190 target.
